feed.title: SAP Community - SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central
feed.subtitle: SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Validation of time off request Workflow approval status in Business Rule
entry.content:
Hi experts

Need to quick advise/suggestion,

We have a requirement, on Unpaid Leave. 

Requirement:- Unpaid leave has to applied when a user exhaust his/her annual leave balance. If a person is applying for "Unpaid Leave", system should validate certain checks as,

1. Annual leave balance has to be "0" when "Unpaid Leave" is requested

2. Initiated "Annual Leave" request has to be in "Approved" Status when "Unpaid Leave" is requested. 

If both conditions are not true then system should through error and "Submit" button has to be greyed out else user can submit the request. 

We could able to configure the validation on annual leave balance check but we couldn't able to validate the "Annual Leave" historical transactions to validate the approval status when "Unpaid Leave" is applied.

 

Looking forward for suggestion, is it feasible to configure?

 

Thanks

Arun

entry.title: User Info portlet in the Latest People Profile
entry.content:
Hello,

Can you please advise how we can find or configure the visibility of legacy User Info portlet data in the Latest People Profile?

Thank you.

Best regards,

Martina

entry.title: EC Time Off accrual calculation: consider Saturday on the full monthe of absences
entry.content:
hi, 

in my company we calculate monthly holiday accruals. If there is absences on the entire month there is no accrual calculation. I used the the function "Get Absence In Days For Period Based On Calendar Days For Time Types()" and it works well. 

How ever, if the absence starts on sunday for example, and Saturday is the first day of the month (and same if Saturday is the last day of the month) and saturday is a non working day (according the work schedule), the entire month should be considered. 

here the rule I cretated: 

How Can I make sur to catch the saturdays (the non working days) in my calculation, as currently only the full month of absences are catched?

Thanks in advance for your advice!

Sevda

entry.title: Monthly Time Sheet - Error When employee records more than 20 hours OT
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We come across common requirement to throw error when using time containers, Like after 20 Hours of OT, employee should not be able to submit. Currently we are able to raise the alerts but we need to stop them from proceeding. Is there any possibility to throw error or will be available anytime in future ?

Thanks in Advance!

Thanks,
Vijey

entry.title: Duplicate field is appearing in the Custom Object without config change
entry.content:
Hi,

We have encountered a peculiar problem of Duplicate field appearance in the Custom MDF without making any changes to the Object Configuration or UI. The field started to appear post January 10th. No patch update has happened during that time for SF. Can any one please suggest any method to overcome this issue? or atleast on what basis this duplication occurs. This has come up in our overseas deputation object for only one of the components. Rest others are working fine.

Please any one can help on this or if u have faced any such situation kindly share.

entry.title: To split the workflow based rule into two as the number of workflows in the rule has gone beyond 600
entry.content:
We have a scenario where the workflow based rule is created for Separation module for 32 countries having close to 700 plus workflows segregated based on action types. This is making the rule get overloaded. It takes time to load, to edit and/or make any changes. We still have close to 10 countries to on board for separation module and its making the rule more complicated. Is there a possibility to split the workflow based business rule into two? 

Kindly request you to suggest any solution to overcome this problem.

Or if you have implemented any solution for the same, requesting you to help with that.

entry.title: Effective date when status is "Inactive" for a Department using business rule
entry.content:
Hello Experts,
I hope that you are doing good. Kindly assist if there is any way to pull effective date when status is set as "Inactive" for a department using business rule.
I tried to use look up function using variable but null is being returned.

Requirement is to prompt a message (Department will be inactive from date XXXXX) while saving Job Information if department has any future dated record with inactive status.

Thanks,
R

entry.title: Directory Search in SuccessFactors - Any roadmap?
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We are wondering if there any plans from SuccesFactors side to extend the functionalities of the New Directory Search? Is there any roadmap available for (missing) functions like multi-selection filtering or making available more standard and/or custom fields for filtering?

Thanks a lot in advance,
Nina

entry.title: Pay slip dispay in EC using mash-ups
entry.content:
Dear all,

If customer would like to use mash-ups to display Pay slips in SF EC for employees (Payroll is run on S/4), how is it possible to control to dispay only last payroll results? In case of several payroll runs to correct the errors, how to prevent to see them all?

Thank you,

Jasminka

entry.title: I am trying to change referral bonus on the job classification in manage data
entry.content: I did a mass load to Job Classification data, but the Referral Bonus value didn't populate. The object definition for Job Classification includes a custom field for Referral Bonus (picklist). I have already added the value to the picklist.  How do I populate the Referral Bonus on Job Classification so it carries over to Position?  Any help would be appreciated.

entry.title: Difference between job type "Synch HRIS data" and "HRIS Sync"
entry.content:
We noticed that there are two different job types referring to HRIS Synchronization. Question is what is the difference between those? We noticed that one job type is being triggered by Provisioning and one from the instance.

Is there a different scope for those job types? If yes which are those?

 

Thanks in advance for your input.

entry.title: Facing below error Inbound processing in endpoint at /EC_to_ERP_Generic_Object_Query
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

I am facing below error in Standard Replication iFlow in SAP CPI.

Inbound processing in endpoint at /EC_to_ERP_Generic_Object_Query failed with message "NullPointerException:while trying to invoke the method org.apache.catalina.TomcatPrincipal.getUserPrincipal() of a null object loaded from field org.apache.catalina.connector.Request.userPrincipal of an object loaded from local variable 'this'"

Please advice on the root cause and any steps need to be taken?

entry.title: Employee Central: hide the effective date
entry.content:
Hello experts,

Please, how to hide the "effective as of" from some users:

entry.title: SuccessFactors Mashup Issue
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a SuccessFactors Employee Central (EC) to SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP) setup for Canada payroll. When we try to save changes in Tax/Garnishment infotypes from EC to ECP using the standard mashup screen in EC, the system displays an error message '500 Internal Server Error. A request for server was sent to server'. See attached error message description.

We reviewed the resolution under SAP note 2887555, but there is no '#same-site-by-default-cookies' option available to review and disable in chrome and microsoft edge browsers.

Has anyone encountered this issue in the past or currently using mashups without any issues?

Thanks,

Mitesh

entry.title: Why can I find an employee/user in "hire date correction" field but not elsewhere in EC?
entry.content:
UPDATE: issue is solved. 

Missing the right permissions was the issue. I had to use DRTM master data with first soft and then hard purge the user. After doing this, the user no longer existed in the search-bar for hire-date.

 

I have a strange situation, where I need to delete a user from EC, because the flow from recruiting to onboarding did not go smooth, due to change of hire date in the future. The employee had initially start date February 1, but the actual first day was February 10. Somehow my team managed to change the hiring-date from 2/1/2025 to 2/10/2025, but then the employee "disappeared" in EC.

We searched after the employee in recruiting, org.chart, search-bar, onboarding and reporting, but we could not find the person until we used "hire date correction" - then the employees name showed but without much data. 

We now want to start the process from scratch with this employee (meaning creating the employee again), but first we must delete the persons data entirely from EC. Due to error message from SF stating "employee data already exists", when we try to create the employee again, this is not possible.

To sum up: How can we delete this persons data for good, when we can't find the employee in purge/data retention management?

entry.title: Employee Central source of data
entry.content:
Hello 

I am not familiar on SAP Success factor Employee Central, I just want to know where the records in employee central coming from is it connected to SAP ECC/S4HANA database/tables?

entry.title: Requirement Gathering Questionnare for SAP SuccessFactors SP
entry.content:
Dear all,

Do you have list of questions that should be asked by SAP SuccessFactors Consultants to implement Succession Planning? Please list here asap.

 

Thanks

entry.title: SuccessFactors Employee Central integration with third party Payroll
entry.content:
Hi Expect,

We recently integrated Employee Central, our core HR system, with a third-party payroll system, which is not SAP Employee Central payroll. We are facing massive problems, can you help indicate the appropriate Implementation guide for third-party payroll reference from SAP?

We are using the Replication of Employee Data from SAP ERP HCM to employee central using SAP cloud integration as Middleware.

Regards

Carolina

entry.title: How to write the formula in Bus rule where the notice PD is >6 months even for 6months and 1 day?
entry.content:
We have a requirement to put in place where the notice period duration for a country is 1 month if the employee tenure/duration in the company is more than 6 months, ie 6 months and 1 day also. If tenure is less than 6 months into the organization, then the notice period should be 1 week.
While using the predefined function "get the number of months between start and end dates", where my start date is hire date and end date is current date, then the function behaves like if the tenure in company is equal to 6 or <6 months then the notice period is picked up as defined ie. 1 week.

 

When the duration is more than 6 months, then the function will consider the 1 month notice period only if the month has passed. Ex: if tenure is 6 months and 5 days, then the function picks up the 1 week condition. If the tenure is 7 months (post 6 months), then it considers the current condition or 1 month condition. 

now my problem is, even if the tenure is 6 months and 2 days, the 1 month condition should be picked up and not 1 week. 

Request anyone to help me on this issue. Business people are surprised on listening to the way SF is calculating the number of months. We should have an option to input decimal value also in Business rule similar to number. Kindly help if anyone has been able to implement similar requirement.

Regards,

Nagavalli B n

entry.title: Work permit alert expiration rule issue
entry.content:
Hello SAP Experts,

I was trying to create a business rule to trigger an alert notification and send an email to inform HR that an employee’s Work Permit in Singapore will expire in 30 days and 60 days.

For this purpose, I created the following rule:

However, after reviewing the workflow and the alert message, HR only receives the alert when the Work Permit expiration date is two months before. The second condition for 30 days is being skipped.
In Manage Alerts & Notifications, the 30-day alert remains pending.

I tried a different approach, and my concern is whether this approach is correct, using a date plus condition in the "IF" clause:



With this second approach, HR receives both alerts for 30 days and 60 days, and the alert creation date and alert effective date remain the same (as of today, during testing).

Could you please provide your expertise and guidance?

Thank you!
